We cannot prevent every wildfire, but we can reduce their severity through responsible, science-based forest management. Healthy forests are good for our environment, our economy, and public health.

We see this firsthand in @NatResources.

Too often, in both the United States and Canada, active forest management has been sidelined by green lobby policies that lock up forests, allow dangerous fuel to accumulate, and increase the risk of larger, more destructive wildfires.

🧵An Air Quality Advisory is once again in effect across Wisconsin as smoke from wildfires in northern Minnesota and Canada drifts into our state.
